1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 "Controller" means the entity that determines the purposes

attempts.claim: If the Controller objects to a proposed Sub-Processor within ten days, the Processor is required to immediately terminate any relationship with that Sub-Processor and may not proceed with the engagement.

"Personal Data" as defined in the contract includes any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural or legal person processed by the Processor on behalf of the Controller under the Agreement.
The DPA requires the Processor to notify affected Data Subjects directly in the event of a Data Breach affecting their Personal Data.
Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, the Processor is required to delete all Personal Data without exception.
The contract sets an overall maximum liability cap for the Processor of EUR 1,000,000 for all claims arising under both the Agreement and this DPA combined.

results.document_preview:
MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is enter

attempts.claim: Upon termination of the Agreement, all Deliverables and associated Intellectual Property Rights automatically revert to the Service Provider regardless of payment status.

Under this Agreement, 'Confidential Information' includes all information exchanged between the parties, regardless of whether it is marked as confidential or could reasonably be understood to be confidential.
Upon termination of this Agreement, Service Provider is obligated to return or destroy all Confidential Information belonging to the Client within 30 days.
All Intellectual Property Rights in the Deliverables are automatically assigned to the Client upon delivery.
The maximum total liability of either party under this Agreement is $500,000 in aggregate for all claims arising during the term.

results.document_preview:
1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Agreement” means this SaaS Subscription Agreement.  
1.2 “

attempts.claim: Upon termination of the Agreement due to Customer's breach, Provider is entitled to retain all Customer Data and is not obligated to provide access to such data.
The term 'Customer Data' includes all data generated by the Service during Customer's use, regardless of whether it was uploaded or processed by Customer or its users.
Upon termination of the Agreement, Provider is obligated to permanently delete all Customer Data from its systems within thirty (30) days after making it available for download.
If Provider does not meet the 99.5% Monthly Uptime Percentage in any month, Customer is automatically entitled to receive a service credit equal to at least 5% of the monthly Fees.
The maximum total amount of service credits that Customer can receive under this Agreement for SLA failures in a single calendar year is limited to 20% of the total annual Fees paid.

results.document_preview:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (“Agreemen

attempts.claim: If the Receiving Party is required by law to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so without notifying the Disclosing Party if the disclosure is solely to its Representatives for the Permitted Purpose.

Under this Agreement, information that is independently developed by the Receiving Party but is similar or identical to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information is still considered Confidential Information and subject to all confidentiality obligations.
The Agreement requires both Parties to notify each other immediately if they become aware of any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information.

The Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief and other equitable remedies for any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information, without the necessity of posting bond or proving actual damages.

The Agreement requires that all Confidential Information, including archival copies, must be returned or destroyed within a total of thirty (30) business days of the Disclosing Party’s written request.

results.document_preview:
1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 "Controller" means the entity that determines the purposes

attempts.claim: If Controller objects to a new Sub-Processor under clause 3.1(b), Processor is required to immediately terminate the Agreement as stipulated in section 7.

"Personal Data" as defined in this DPA includes any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person that is processed by either the Controller or the Processor under the Agreement, regardless of which party processes it.
Under this DPA, the Controller is required to approve all Sub-Processors before the Processor can engage them to process Personal Data.
The Controller has the right to prevent the appointment of any new Sub-Processor by objecting within ten days of notice.
The Processor is required to notify the Controller of any Data Breach affecting Personal Data within a cumulative maximum of 48 hours per calendar year, regardless of the number of incidents.

results.document_preview:
MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is enter

attempts.claim: Upon termination of the Agreement, all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables automatically revert to the Service Provider, regardless of payment status.
Under this Agreement, 'Personal Data' includes any information processed by Service Provider, regardless of whether it relates to an identified or identifiable natural person.

Upon termination of this Agreement, the Service Provider is obligated to transfer all Personal Data processed on behalf of the Client back to the Client and permanently delete any remaining copies from its systems.
The Client automatically owns all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables created by the Service Provider under this Agreement.
The maximum total liability of either party under this Agreement, including all SOWs over the entire Term, is capped at $250,000 in aggregate.

results.document_preview:
1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Agreement” means this SaaS Subscription Agreement.  
1.2 “

attempts.claim: If the Provider fails to meet the Monthly Uptime Percentage for three consecutive months, the Customer may terminate the Agreement immediately without notice.
The term 'Authorized Users' includes any third-party consultants hired by the Customer's clients, as long as they are granted access by the Customer.
Upon termination of the Agreement, Provider is obligated to permanently delete all copies of Customer Data from its systems within thirty (30) days after making the data available for download.

If the Provider fails to meet the 99.5% Monthly Uptime Percentage in any calendar month, the Customer is automatically entitled to a service credit of 5% of the monthly Fees for each 1% shortfall, up to 20%, without any additional action required.

The maximum total amount of service credits that the Customer can receive under this Agreement over the entire Subscription Term is capped at 20% of one month’s Fees, regardless of the number of SLA violations or months affected.

results.document_preview:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (“Agreemen

attempts.claim: The Agreement allows the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives even after the expiration or termination of the Agreement, provided that the Representatives use the information solely for the Permitted Purpose.

Under the Agreement, 'Representatives' also includes contractors and consultants engaged by a Party, provided they are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those in the Agreement.
If a Party's Representative breaches the confidentiality obligations, the Agreement automatically deems the Party itself to be in breach, regardless of the Party's efforts to ensure compliance.
The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief without any need to provide notice to the Receiving Party.
The total maximum period during which any Confidential Information must be protected under this Agreement is eight years from the Effective Date.
